prompts.text: Explain to a curious interviewer what your team actually does all day.
Explain to your skip-level what your current project is about.
Explain to a new hire how decisions actually get made at this company.
Explain to a non-technical client what it is you're building for them.
Explain to your parents what your job title means.
Explain to a cross-functional partner what your team's quarterly goal is.
Explain to your manager why this week's deliverable slipped.
Explain to a panel interviewer what kind of work you do best.
Explain to the board what the company's biggest risk is right now.
Explain to a hostile reporter what last week's outage really meant.
Should we hire the candidate everyone on the panel disagreed about?
Did your team hit the quarterly goal or not?
Is your direct report ready for the promotion?
Should we accept the client's scope-creep request?
Did the launch hit what we said it would?
Should we delay the all-hands until we have more answers?
Is the cross-functional partnership actually working?
Should I take the offer at the other company?
Should the board approve the new operating plan?
Should we settle the customer complaint or escalate it?
Explain to a new hire how your team prioritizes work each week.
Explain to a non-technical client what trade-offs we made on their project.
Explain to your manager how your role has actually grown this year.
Explain to an interviewer how you'd approach a brand-new problem.
Explain to a peer what your team owns and where the seams are.
Explain to an exec why a small change will cost a whole quarter.
Explain to a cross-functional partner why their request is blocked.
Explain to your skip-level why morale on your team is slipping.
Explain to an investor why the next 12 months matter more than the last 12.
Explain to a journalist what your company actually sells, in one breath.
Explain your project's status to your grandmother.
Explain what an OKR is to a brand new employee.

Explain a performance improvement plan to the report receiving it.
Explain your team's quarterly results to a finance partner.
Explain what a postmortem is to someone outside the company.

Explain an offsite agenda to your CEO without buzzwords.
Explain a layoff package to the employees receiving it.
Explain a board-level strategy doc to the whole company at an all-hands.
Why should your manager believe you're ready for a senior title?
Why should the hiring panel pick you over the other finalist?
Why should the client trust your team after the last delay?
Why is your team's roadmap the right one for next quarter?
Why should your skip-level fund the new headcount you're asking for?
Why is the cross-functional partner's request actually a bad idea?
Why should your direct report take the stretch project they're avoiding?
Why is now the right time to ask for a raise?
Why should an investor bet on your team versus the obvious incumbent?
Why should the company keep the project everyone wants to kill?
What's the single reason your team missed the goal this quarter?
What's the one thing your direct report needs to change?
What's the one reason this client is unhappy?
Why is the cross-functional partnership stuck?
What's the one thing the candidate did better than the others?
What's the single biggest reason you'd take this new job offer?
What's the one thing your skip-level should know that no one's telling them?
What's the one reason your team's morale is slipping?
What's the single biggest risk to the company in the next 18 months?
Why is this the right moment to pivot the whole product?
Explain the difference between a manager and a leader.
Explain the difference between a goal and an OKR to a new hire.
Explain the difference between feedback and a performance plan.
Explain the difference between strategy and execution to your team.
Explain why missing a deadline is not the same as failing.
Explain the difference between a 1:1 and a status check to a new manager.

Explain to an exec the difference between burnout and disengagement.
Explain to the board why revenue and retention can move in opposite directions.
Explain to the all-hands why a layoff isn't proof the strategy is wrong.
Tell me why you deserve the promotion you're asking for.
Tell me why this is the right next role for your career.
Tell me why your direct report should keep their job after this incident.
Tell me why the client should renew despite this quarter's misses.
Tell me why your team is the right one to own this initiative.
Tell me why a peer should take the harder side of a tradeoff with you.
Tell me why this quarter is going to land differently than last.
Tell me why the company should keep investing in your project.
Tell me why this is the company an investor should back.
Tell me why you'd run the company differently if it were yours.
Pitch yourself in 30 words to an interviewer.
Sum up your career arc for a new manager.
Describe your team's quarterly goal in one breath.

Give three reasons you're the right hire for this role.
Make the case in three points for the promotion you want.
Argue why your team needs to grow by two people.
Defend in three points why your project deserves more time.
Argue why this client should sign a multi-year extension.
Make the case to your manager for switching teams.
Justify killing the project everyone on your team loves.
Convince a cross-functional partner to take on extra scope.
Argue why the company should bet the year on a single big initiative.
Defend a controversial reorg to the all-hands.
Tell me about a time you failed at work.
Walk me through the hardest week of your career so far.
Tell the story of a project that went sideways and how you recovered.

Recount the all-hands where you had to deliver news no one wanted.
Should you take the new job offer or stay where you are?
Promote internally or hire from outside for the open role?
Push the deadline a week or ship what we have?
Tell the client now or wait until we know more?
Should we fire the underperformer or coach them another quarter?
Reorg the team this quarter or wait until next?
Should you ask for the raise now or wait for review cycle?
Tell the team about the layoff today or after the all-hands?
Raise the next round now or wait twelve months for better metrics?
Settle the lawsuit or fight it?
Your team's morale dropped after the reorg. Address it.
Standups are running 45 minutes. Fix it.
Your inbox has 4,000 unread messages. Walk me through your plan.
Two senior peers won't speak to each other. Resolve it.
The client just said they're considering a competitor. Respond.
Your top performer just told you they're interviewing elsewhere. Address it.
Cross-functional partners keep blocking your team's launches. Solve it.
Your skip-level just questioned the value of your team. Respond.

A memo about company culture just leaked. Address the press.

Persuade your manager to let you lead the new initiative.

Persuade your skip-level to back a controversial reorg.
Convince the all-hands to keep showing up after a round of layoffs.
Rally a board to back you over an activist investor's plan.

Working at a startup vs working at a big company — for your next role.
Staying in your current role vs taking the lateral move.
Building the team vs hiring an agency for this project.
A direct conversation vs writing a memo — for delivering this feedback.
Promote your senior IC vs hire a manager from outside.
Annual planning vs continuous re-planning for your team.
Coaching vs replacing the underperforming peer.

Staying lean vs raising the next round at any valuation.
Selling the company now vs grinding for a bigger outcome in three years.
